old_claim.note: Slide Rule provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The corrected node is anchored to early-1640s astronomical micrometer measurement, which is source-supported as telescope measuring apparatus rather than a slide-rule derivative.

source_shape.source_locator: Linda Hall Library describes Gascoigne's astronomical micrometer as an early-1640s telescope measuring device with an adjustable screw for angular measurements.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Gascoigne's micrometer work belongs to telescope-based angular measurement, not to slide-rule calculation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports a micrometer origin path through telescope instrumentation and does not identify slide rules as a direct prerequisite.

ontology_before: Micrometers & Precision Measurement directly depended on Slide Rule through a broad calculation-instrument inference.
ontology_after: Micrometers & Precision Measurement is scoped to precision instrument measurement and no longer depends directly on slide-rule calculation.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-22-renaissance-source-check-scope keeps slide_rule absent as a direct prerequisite for micrometers_precision_measurement.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to slide-rule manufacturing or calculator-scale micrometry rather than telescope measurement.
A source showed slide rules were a direct prerequisite for Gascoigne-style micrometer measurement.
short_reason: The source-backed micrometer path is telescope instrumentation, not slide-rule calculation.
